div.p:
Key Insight: The "indexes are always faster"
misconception is dangerous. An index on gender
(50% M, 50% F) is useless for WHERE gender = 'M'
— sequential scan is 5-10x faster.

ul.li: Reads entire table regardless of selectivity

div.p: cost = (sel × pages) + (sel × rows × cpu_w) + index_height × lookups

ul.li:
Below: IndexScan cheaper
Few rows = few random I/Os
Index overhead amortized

Above: SeqScan cheaper
Random I/O dominates
Sequential scan wins
